english: pf. smad (smad), Lex. v. smád-pa (smad pa), to blame, bdag stod γźan smod (bdag stod gzhan smod) to praise one's self, disparaging others; rźogs-smód byéd-pa (rzhogs smod byed pa) to slander, calumniate Thgy.; to depreciate, to make contemptible, smód-par gyúr-bas (smod par gyur bas) Stg. because it would be disreputable, would detract from his honour. For smod-_dzúg-pa (smod 'dzug pa) it would prob. be better to write dmod-_dzug-pa (dmod 'dzug pa).

to wish, to desire, with la, skyíd-pa γźán-la ṅa mi smon (skyid pa gzhan la nga mi smon) for another happiness I do not wish Mil.; more frq. with termin. of the infinitive, and then = to pray for, rgyál-po skyé-bar (rgyal po skye bar) (to pray for) being re-born as a king Dzl.; smón-pa bźín-du byéd-pa (smon pa bzhin du byed pa) to fulfil a prayer Dzl.; smón-pai gnas (smon pa'i gnas) the object of a wish or prayer Cs.; yid-smón (yid smon) wish, desire, de tsúr-śog-gi yid-smon ṅá-la med (de tshur shog gi yid smon nga la med) I do not wish that he should come Mil.; riṅ-po-nas _di-lta-bur yid-smon byéd-par gyúr-te (ring po nas 'di lta bur yid smon byed par gyur te) having long ago entertained this wish Stg.; yid-smon _os (yid smon 'os) worth wishing, desirable; smon-_júg (smon 'jug) a wish and its accomplishment, smon-_júg γñyis (smon 'jug gnyis); smón-lam (smon lam), prayer, whether it be in the general way of expressing a good wish or offering a petition to the deity, or in the specific Brahmanic-Buddhistic form, which is always united with some condition or asseveration, as: if such or such a thing be true, then may ..., wishing-prayer. — smon-(lam) lóg(-par) _débs-pa (smon (lam) log (-par) 'debs pa) to curse, to execrate.
comrade, companion, associate, = grógs-po (grogs po) Lex.

Sch.: smyan byéd-pa (smyan byed pa) to travel on business; smyan-byed blo-źan (smyan byed blo zhan) a traveling clerk not very shrewd Bhar. 108; this would seem preferable to the Ssk. equivalent, mentioned in Schf.'s edition.
Sch. to stretch one's self, after sleep.
thin cane, writing-cane, reed-pen; *doi nyi-gu* (*doi nyi gu*) C. goose-quill, *càg-gi nyi-gu* (*cag gi nyi gu*) C. steel-pen.

1. cane, bamboo, smyúg-mai sbubs (smyug ma'i sbubs) tube of bamboo Cs. — 2. a pen of reed, _jóg-pu ('jog pu), W. *źóg-če* (*zhog che*) to make a reed-pen; *di-nyúg* (*di nyug*) id., improp. also lead-pencil. Comp. smyug-króg (smyug khrog) Cs., acc. to others, smyug-sbróg (smyug sbrog) tube of bamboo; pen-case; small churn, = gur-gúr (gur gur) Cs. — *nyug-kyím* (*nyug khyim*) C. house constructed of bamboo. — smyúg-mkhan (smyug mkhan) a worker in cane Cs. — smyug-sgám (smyug sgam) a chest made of reed Cs. — smyug-grí (smyug gri) pen-knife. — smyug-lcág (smyug lcag) flag, flag-stick; long bamboo Cs. — *nyug-tál* (*nyug thal*) C. a flat basket. — smyug-tógs (smyug thogs) writer Cs. — smyug-dóṅ (smyug dong) Cs. = gur-gur (gur gur). — smyug-gdán (smyug gdan) mat of reed, cane-mat. — smyug-gdúgs (smyug gdugs) an umbrella made of split reeds Cs. — smyug-sdér (smyug sder) plate, dish or flat basket, constructed of reed C. — *nyug-tsá-me-tog* (*nyug tsa me tog*) C., Carthusian pink. — smyug-tsígs (smyug tshigs) knot, node, joint, of reeds. — *nyug-lóm* (*nyug lom*) C. flat basket. — smyug-bśád (smyug bshad) comb made of bamboo.

to fast, to observe a strict diet Med.; often in a religious sense, smyúṅ-bar byás-pa (smyung bar byas pa) and ma byas-pa (ma byas pa) he who has strictly observed fasting, and he who has not Do.; smyuṅ-gnás (smyung gnas) the fast, the act of fasting; *nyéṅ-ṅe nyúṅ-ṅe zúm-če* (*nyeng nge nyung nge zum che*) W. to fast, to practise abstinence. V. Schl. 240.
to be quick, expeditious, in a hurry, to hasten Cs. Cf. myúr-ba (myur ba).

pf. smyos (smyos), myos (myos) to be insane, mad, či-_aṅ mi drán-par myós-so (ci 'ang mi dran par myos so) they lost their senses and ran mad (with grief) Dzl.; smyos-sam (smyos sam) is she mad? Dzl.; sñyiṅ myós-pas (snying myos pas) Do., being deranged; *nyo dug* (*nyo dug*) W. he is crazy; to be mad, as dogs Schr.; to be intoxicated, smyó-bai kǔ-ba (smyo ba'i khu ba) intoxicating liquor Dzl.; rtág-tu myós-pai gnas (rtag tu myos pa'i gnas) pot-houses, fuddling-places Stg.; fig. _dod-čágs-kyis myós ('dod chags kyis myos) Dzl. he is mad with lust; smyó-bar byéd-pa (smyo bar byed pa) to make one mad or drunk. — smyo-byéd (smyo byed) 1. narcotic, smyo-byéd-kyi rdzas (smyo byed kyi rdzas) narcotic medicine, soporiferous potion, maddening drink. 2. smyo-byed(-kyi) gdon (smyo byed (-kyi) gdon) a demon that causes a state <
